 71 |         - toConfig(newRules) preserves the order within this request, but Config.updateGlobal() merges those keys into the existing permission.<tool> object. Because evaluate() later uses findLast(), a newly added broad rule like "git *" will be appended after any older specific rules such as "git log *" and start overriding them on the next load. File: docs/introduction.md
Lines 1-166:
  1 | # Workspace Introduction
  2 | 
  3 | > **For agents** — Read this file first to understand the workspace layout before starting any task.
  4 | > This is a living document; update it as the workspace evolves.
  5 | 
  6 | ---
  7 | 
  8 | ## Workspace: `kilo-org`
  9 | 
 10 | This workspace contains all repositories and notes related to the **Kilo Code** platform — an AI-powered agentic engineering tool. Below is a brief description of each top-level directory.
 11 | 
 12 | ---
 13 | 
 14 | ### `cloud/`
 15 | 
 16 | Backend monorepo for the Kilo Code platform. Built with **Next.js** (deployed on Vercel) and a constellation of **Cloudflare Workers** (cloud-agent, db-proxy, gastown, security-sync, auto-fix, auto-triage, deploy-infra, etc.). Contains the web dashboard, API routes, authentication, billing, and all cloud-side infrastructure that powers the product. Uses **pnpm** as package manager.
 17 | 
 18 | ---
 19 | 
 20 | ### `kilocode/`
 21 | 
 22 | Primary monorepo for the Kilo Code client-side product. A **Bun/Turbo** monorepo with packages for the **VS Code extension**, **desktop app**, **CLI**, **web UI**, **plugin system**, **SDKs**, **i18n**, **telemetry**, and shared utilities. This is the active repository where all new feature development happens. Uses **bun** as package manager.
 23 | 
 24 | #### Architecture Overview
 25 | 
 26 | All products are thin clients over the **CLI** (`packages/opencode/`, published as `@kilocode/cli`). The CLI is a fork of upstream [OpenCode](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode) with Kilo-specific additions (gateway auth, telemetry, migration, code review, branding). It contains the full AI agent runtime, tool execution, session management, provider integrations (500+ models), and an HTTP API server.
 27 | 
 28 | Every client spawns or connects to a `kilo serve` process and communicates via HTTP REST + SSE using the auto-generated `@kilocode/sdk`.
 29 | 
 30 | ```
 31 |                         @kilocode/cli  (packages/opencode/)
 32 |                      ┌────────────────────────────────┐
 33 |                      │  AI agents, tools, sessions,    │
 34 |                      │  providers, config, MCP, LSP    │
 35 |                      │  Hono HTTP server + SSE         │
 36 |                      └──┬──────────┬──────────┬───────┘
 37 |                         │          │          │
 38 |                 ┌───────┴──┐ ┌────┴────┐ ┌───┴──────────┐
 39 |                 │ TUI      │ │ VS Code │ │ Desktop / Web│
 40 |                 │ (builtin)│ │Extension│ │              │
 41 |                 └──────────┘ └─────────┘ └──────────────┘
 42 | ```
 43 | 
 44 | #### Packages
 45 | 
 46 | | Package | Name | Role |
 47 | |---|---|---|
 48 | | `packages/opencode/` | `@kilocode/cli` | **Core CLI** — AI agents, tools, sessions, Hono HTTP server, TUI. Fork of upstream OpenCode. |
 49 | | `packages/kilo-vscode/` | `kilo-code` | **VS Code extension** — sidebar chat + Agent Manager. Bundles CLI binary. |
 50 | | `packages/kilo-gateway/` | `@kilocode/kilo-gateway` | Auth (device flow), AI provider routing (OpenRouter), Kilo API integration |
 51 | | `packages/kilo-ui/` | `@kilocode/kilo-ui` | SolidJS component library (40+ components, built on `@kobalte/core`) |
 52 | | `packages/kilo-telemetry/` | `@kilocode/kilo-telemetry` | PostHog analytics + OpenTelemetry tracing |
 53 | | `packages/kilo-i18n/` | `@kilocode/kilo-i18n` | Translation strings (16 languages) |
 54 | | `packages/kilo-docs/` | `@kilocode/kilo-docs` | Documentation site (Next.js + Markdoc) |
 55 | | `packages/sdk/js/` | `@kilocode/sdk` | Auto-generated TypeScript SDK client for the server API |
 56 | | `packages/app/` | `@opencode-ai/app` | Shared SolidJS web UI for desktop app and `kilo web` |
 57 | | `packages/desktop/` | `@opencode-ai/desktop` | Tauri desktop app shell |
 58 | | `packages/ui/` | `@opencode-ai/ui` | Shared UI primitives + 75+ custom SVG icons |
 59 | | `packages/util/` | `@opencode-ai/util` | Shared utilities (error, path, retry, slug) |
 60 | | `packages/plugin/` | `@kilocode/plugin` | Plugin/tool interface definitions |
 61 | | `packages/storybook/` | — | Storybook for component development |
 62 | | `packages/containers/` | — | Container definitions |
 63 | | `packages/extensions/` | — | Extension-related utilities |
 64 | | `packages/script/` | `@opencode-ai/script` | Build/release scripts |
 65 | 
 66 | ---
 67 | 
 68 | #### `packages/kilo-vscode/` — VS Code Extension (Deep Dive)
 69 | 
 70 | The VS Code extension is a thin client that bundles its own CLI binary at `bin/kilo`. At startup it spawns `bin/kilo serve --port 0`, captures the dynamically-assigned port from stdout, and communicates over HTTP + SSE. A random password is generated and passed via `KILO_SERVER_PASSWORD` env var for basic auth.
 71 | 
 72 | **Key source files:**
 73 | 
 74 | | File | Role |
 75 | |---|---|
 76 | | `src/extension.ts` | Entry point — creates `KiloConnectionService`, registers providers, autocomplete, code actions, browser automation |
 77 | | `src/KiloProvider.ts` | Main webview provider for the sidebar (81KB — handles all message routing between extension and webview) |
 78 | | `src/services/cli-backend/connection-service.ts` | Singleton shared across all webviews — owns server process, HTTP client, SSE connection |
 79 | | `src/services/cli-backend/server-manager.ts` | Spawns the CLI binary and manages process lifecycle |
 80 | | `src/services/cli-backend/sdk-sse-adapter.ts` | Adapts SDK SSE events for the extension |
 81 | | `src/agent-manager/AgentManagerProvider.ts` | Agent Manager editor panel — multi-session orchestration with git worktree isolation |
 82 | | `src/DiffViewerProvider.ts` | Standalone diff viewer for "Show Changes" action |
 83 | | `src/SettingsEditorProvider.ts` | Settings/profile editor (opens in editor area) |
 84 | | `src/SubAgentViewerProvider.ts` | Sub-agent viewer |
 85 | | `src/services/autocomplete/` | Inline autocomplete system (classic + chat-based, with tree-sitter, continuedev integration) |
 86 | | `src/services/browser-automation/` | Playwright MCP registration for browser automation |
 87 | | `src/services/code-actions/` | VS Code code action providers + terminal actions |
 88 | | `src/services/commit-message/` | AI-generated commit messages |
 89 | 
 90 | **Webview UI** (`webview-ui/`):
 91 | - Built with **Solid.js** (not React) — JSX compiles via `esbuild-plugin-solid`
 92 | - Three separate webview builds: sidebar (`webview-ui/src/`), Agent Manager (`webview-ui/agent-manager/`), diff viewer (`webview-ui/diff-viewer/`)
 93 | - Uses `@kilocode/kilo-ui` component library
 94 | - Provider hierarchy: `ThemeProvider → I18nProvider → DialogProvider → MarkedProvider → VSCodeProvider → ServerProvider → ProviderProvider → SessionProvider`
 95 | - Components organized in: `chat/`, `history/`, `migration/`, `profile/`, `settings/`, `shared/`
 96 | 
 97 | **Agent Manager** — opens as editor tab (`Cmd+Shift+M`), provides multi-session orchestration with up to 4 parallel worktrees, each optionally isolated in its own git worktree. All sessions share the single `kilo serve` process.
 98 | 
 99 | **Build system** — Two separate esbuild builds in `esbuild.js`:
100 | - Extension (Node/CJS): `src/extension.ts` → `dist/extension.js`
101 | - Webview (browser/IIFE): `webview-ui/src/index.tsx` → `dist/webview.js` AND `webview-ui/agent-manager/index.tsx` → `dist/agent-manager.js`
102 | 
103 | ---
104 | 
105 | #### `packages/opencode/` — CLI / Server (Deep Dive)
106 | 
107 | The core engine of Kilo Code. A fork of upstream [OpenCode](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode) with Kilo-specific additions in `src/kilocode/`. Contains the AI agent runtime, tool execution, session management, provider integrations, and an HTTP API server.
108 | 
109 | **Key patterns:**
110 | - **Namespace modules** — Code organized as TypeScript namespaces (not classes), each exporting Zod schemas, types, and functions
111 | - **`Instance.state(init, dispose?)`** — Per-project lazy singleton tied to project directory via `AsyncLocalStorage`
112 | - **`fn(schema, callback)`** — Wraps functions with Zod input validation
113 | - **`Tool.define(id, init)`** — All tools follow this pattern returning `{ description, parameters, execute }`
114 | - **`BusEvent.define(type, schema)` + `Bus.publish()`** — In-process pub/sub event system
115 | - **`NamedError.create(name, schema)`** — Structured errors with Zod schemas
116 | - **Storage** — Filesystem-based JSON in `~/.local/share/kilo/storage/` (keys are path arrays)
117 | 
118 | **Source modules (`src/`):**
119 | 
120 | | Module | Purpose |
121 | |---|---|
122 | | `server/` | Hono HTTP server with OpenAPI spec, SSE streaming, 15+ route files (session, config, provider, mcp, file, pty, etc.) |
123 | | `agent/` | AI agent definitions and prompt templates |
124 | | `session/` | Session management, message processing, LLM interaction, prompt construction (70KB prompt.ts), compaction, retry, revert |
125 | | `tool/` | 20+ built-in tools: `bash`, `edit`, `read`, `write`, `apply_patch`, `grep`, `glob`, `ls`, `lsp`, `websearch`, `webfetch`, `task`, `plan`, `todo`, `question`, `batch`, `codesearch`, `multiedit`, `skill`, `diagnostics` |
126 | | `provider/` | AI provider integrations via Vercel AI SDK — model cache, provider registry, transform layer, 1.5MB models snapshot |
127 | | `config/` | Configuration management |
128 | | `permission/` | Permission system (arity, next-action resolution) |
129 | | `mcp/` | Model Context Protocol integration |
130 | | `lsp/` | Language Server Protocol integration |
131 | | `snapshot/` | File snapshot/checkpoint system |
132 | | `shell/` | Shell command execution |
133 | | `pty/` | Pseudo-terminal management |
134 | | `worktree/` | Git worktree management |
135 | | `project/` | Project instance management, VCS integration, bootstrap |
136 | | `auth/` | Authentication |
137 | | `bus/` | Event bus system |
138 | | `storage/` | Database (Drizzle ORM) + JSON file storage |
139 | | `cli/cmd/` | CLI commands: `serve`, `run`, `auth`, `agent`, `mcp`, `models`, `pr`, `session`, `export`, `import`, `stats`, `upgrade`, `uninstall`, `db`, `acp` |
140 | | `cli/cmd/tui/` | Terminal UI built with SolidJS + OpenTUI (`@opentui/solid`) |
141 | | `kilocode/` | Kilo-specific additions: config injection, enhance-prompt, review system, migration utilities, TUI components, cloud session integration |
142 | 
143 | **Server routes** (`src/server/routes/`): `session`, `config`, `provider`, `mcp`, `file`, `pty`, `project`, `global`, `permission`, `question`, `commit-message`, `enhance-prompt`, `telemetry`, `tui`, `workspace`, `experimental`
144 | 
145 | **Fork management** — Kilo-specific changes in shared upstream files are marked with `// kilocode_change` comments. Kilo-only code lives in `src/kilocode/` (no markers needed). Regular merges from upstream OpenCode.
146 | 
147 | ---
148 | 
149 | ### `kilocode-legacy/`
150 | 
151 | Previous monorepo for the Kilo Code VS Code extension (now archived). Contains the original **extension source**, **webview UI**, **JetBrains plugin**, benchmarks, and release tooling. Development has officially moved to `kilocode/` — this repo is kept for reference, history, and any remaining migration work. Uses **pnpm** as package manager.
152 | 
153 | ---
154 | 
155 | ### `work-notes/`
156 | 
157 | Personal working notes, task tracking, and documentation. Contains the active **task list** (`task_list.md`), completed tasks archive (`done.md`, `0-done/`), ideas backlog (`1-ideas/`), development notes (`development/`), engineering posts (`eng_posts/`), and in-depth task analyses (`tareas/`). This is the go-to place for planning, context, and day-to-day work management.
158 | 
159 | ---
160 |

162 |

164 | - The active codebase is in `kilocode/` (not `kilocode-legacy/`).
165 | - Cloud infrastructure lives in `cloud/` — each `cloudflare-*` subdirectory is an independent worker.

There's no "reload in place" mechanism in upstream OpenCode. The `global.disposed` event is specifically designed to trigger a full client-side re-fetch. 57 | 58 | If you want to avoid destroying instances, you'd be diverging from upstream's architecture. File: docs/config-broken/como-funciona-config-en-ambos-proyectos.md
Lines 1-67:
 1 | ## Kilocode Legacy Config Architecture vs OpenCode
 2 | 
 3 | ### Legacy Architecture: No Backend Server, No `disposeAll`
 4 | 
 5 | Kilocode-legacy was a **pure VS Code extension** — no CLI backend, no server process, no `Instance.state()` singletons. The architecture was fundamentally different:
 6 | 
 7 | #### Config Storage: VS Code `globalState` + in-memory cache
 8 | 
 9 | [`ContextProxy`](tmp/kilocode-legacy/src/core/config/ContextProxy.ts:47) wraps `vscode.ExtensionContext.globalState` with an **in-memory cache** (`stateCache`, `secretCache`). On startup, it loads all keys from VS Code's `globalState` into the cache. Reads come from cache, writes go to both cache and `globalState` simultaneously:
10 | 
11 | ```typescript
12 | // ContextProxy.ts:218-224
13 | updateGlobalState<K extends GlobalStateKey>(key: K, value: GlobalState[K]) {
14 |     this.stateCache[key] = value                              // update cache
15 |     return this.originalContext.globalState.update(key, value) // persist to disk
16 | }
17 | ```
18 | 
19 | **No dispose needed** — the cache is always in sync because writes update both cache and storage atomically. There's no "stale cache" problem because there's only one source of truth (the `ContextProxy` instance).
20 | 
21 | #### Config Propagation: Event-driven, not cache-invalidation
22 | 
23 | When settings change, the legacy system uses **events** to notify running tasks:
24 | 
25 | 1. User changes a setting → `ClineProvider` updates `ContextProxy` → calls `postStateToWebview()` to sync the webview
26 | 2. For running tasks, `ClineProvider` emits `ProviderProfileChanged` event
27 | 3. The [`Task`](tmp/kilocode-legacy/src/core/task/Task.ts:830) class listens for this event and calls `updateApiConfiguration()`:
28 | 
29 | ```typescript
30 | // Task.ts:836-841
31 | this.providerProfileChangeListener = async () => {
32 |     const newState = await provider.getState()  // reads fresh from ContextProxy
33 |     if (newState?.apiConfiguration) {
34 |         this.updateApiConfiguration(newState.apiConfiguration)  // hot-swaps the API handler
35 |     }
36 | }
37 | ```
38 | 
39 | 4. [`updateApiConfiguration()`](tmp/kilocode-legacy/src/core/task/Task.ts:1728) **hot-swaps the API handler in-place** without destroying the task:
40 | 
41 | ```typescript
42 | // Task.ts:1728-1737
43 | public updateApiConfiguration(newApiConfiguration: ProviderSettings): void {
44 |     this.apiConfiguration = newApiConfiguration
45 |     this.api = buildApiHandler(this.apiConfiguration)
46 |     // IMPORTANT: Do NOT change the parser — tool protocol is locked at creation time
47 | }
48 | ```
49 | 
50 | #### Auto-Approval: Simple boolean flags, read fresh each time
51 | 
52 | Auto-approval settings (`alwaysAllowReadOnly`, `alwaysAllowWrite`, etc.) are stored as individual `globalState` keys. The [`AutoApprovalHandler`](tmp/kilocode-legacy/src/core/auto-approval/AutoApprovalHandler.ts:21) receives the current `state` as a parameter on each check — it reads fresh from `ContextProxy` every time, so changes take effect immediately without any cache invalidation.
53 | 
54 | ### Why OpenCode Needs `disposeAll` and Legacy Didn't
55 | 
56 | | Aspect | Legacy (Kilocode) | OpenCode (Current) |
57 | |---|---|---|
58 | | **Config storage** | VS Code `globalState` with write-through cache | JSON files on disk, read into `Instance.state()` singletons |
59 | | **Config reads** | Always fresh from `ContextProxy` cache (updated on write) | Cached in `Instance.state()` — computed once, never re-read |
60 | | **Running tasks** | Event-driven hot-swap (`updateApiConfiguration`) | No hot-swap — state is frozen at creation |
61 | | **Settings change** | Immediate — cache is always current | Stale until `Instance.disposeAll()` destroys and recreates |
62 | | **MCP connections** | Managed per-provider, not cached in singletons | Cached in `MCP.state()` with dispose callback |
63 | | **Dispose needed?** | ❌ No — write-through cache + events | ✅ Yes — singletons must be destroyed to pick up changes |
64 | 
65 | ### Key Insight
66 | 
67 | The fundamental difference is that legacy used a **write-through cache** pattern (writes update cache immediately) with **event-driven propagation** to running tasks, while OpenCode uses **compute-once singletons** that can only be refreshed by destroying and recreating them. This is why OpenCode needs `disposeAll` and legacy didn't — it's an architectural constraint of the `Instance.state()` pattern, not a design choice.

 1 | ## Complete Inventory of `Instance.state()` Entries
 2 | 
 3 | ### Category 1: Config-derived caches (NEED refresh on config change)
 4 | 
 5 | These read `Config.get()` at init time and cache the result. They're the reason `disposeAll` exists.
 6 | 
 7 | | State | File | Has Dispose | Reads Config | What it caches | Why it resets | Consequence of NOT resetting |
 8 | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
 9 | | **Config.state** | [`config.ts:85`](packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts:85) | ❌ | Reads all config files from disk | Merged config (global + project + .opencode dirs + env) | Config files changed on disk | `Config.get()` returns stale data. **Root cause** — everything downstream is stale |
10 | | **Agent.state** | [`agent.ts:59`](packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:59) | ✅ | `Config.get()` | Agent/mode definitions + their permission rulesets | New modes added, permissions changed | New modes don't appear, permission changes don't apply to agents |
11 | | **Provider.state** | [`provider.ts:799`](packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts:799) | ✅ | `Config.get()` | Provider list, model list, enabled/disabled, SDK instances | Model/provider config changed | Model changes don't take effect, disabled providers still active |
12 | | **Tool.state** | [`registry.ts:38`](packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts:38) | ❌ | `Config.directories()` | Custom tool definitions from .opencode/tools/ | New tools added | Custom tools don't appear |
13 | | **Skill.state** | [`skill.ts:55`](packages/opencode/src/skill/skill.ts:55) | ✅ | Config dirs | Discovered skills from .kilo/skills/ | Skills installed/removed | New skills don't appear |
14 | | **Command.state** | [`command/index.ts:64`](packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts:64) | ✅ | `Config.get()` | Slash commands (built-in + custom) | Custom commands changed | New slash commands don't appear |
15 | | **Plugin.state** | [`plugin/index.ts:32`](packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:32) | ✅ | `Config.get()` | Loaded plugins + hooks | Plugin config changed | Plugin changes don't take effect |
16 | | **Format.state** | [`format/index.ts:27`](packages/opencode/src/format/index.ts:27) | ✅ | `Config.get()` | Enabled formatters | Formatter config changed | Formatter enable/disable doesn't take effect |
17 | | **MCP.state** | [`mcp/index.ts:185`](packages/opencode/src/mcp/index.ts:185) | ✅ | `Config.get()` | MCP client connections + status | MCP servers added/removed/changed | New MCP servers don't connect, removed ones stay |
18 | | **LSP.state** | [`lsp/index.ts:80`](packages/opencode/src/lsp/index.ts:80) | ✅ | `Config.get()` | LSP server processes + clients | LSP config changed | LSP enable/disable doesn't take effect |
19 | | **FileWatcher.state** | [`file/watcher.ts:47`](packages/opencode/src/file/watcher.ts:47) | ❌ | `Config.get()` | File watcher subscriptions (parcel-watcher) | Ignore patterns changed | New ignore patterns don't apply |
20 | | **TuiConfig.state** | [`config/tui.ts:28`](packages/opencode/src/config/tui.ts:28) | ❌ | TUI config files | TUI-specific config (keybinds, theme) | TUI config changed | TUI config changes don't apply |
21 | | **Auth.state** | [`provider/auth.ts:13`](packages/opencode/src/provider/auth.ts:13) | ✅ | `Plugin.list()` | Auth methods from plugins | Auth plugins changed | New auth methods don't appear |
22 | 
23 | ### Category 2: Runtime state (DO NOT need refresh on config change)
24 | 
25 | These hold in-flight operational state. Destroying them kills active operations.
26 | 
27 | | State | File | Has Dispose | What it caches | Why it resets | Consequence of resetting (the DAMAGE) |
28 | |---|---|---|---|---|---|
29 | | **Permission.state** | [`permission/index.ts:53`](packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts:53) | ✅ | Pending permission requests + session approvals | Clean up pending requests | **Kills pending permission prompts** — agents waiting for approval get rejected |
30 | | **PermissionNext.state** | [`permission/next.ts:155`](packages/opencode/src/permission/next.ts:155) | ✅ | Pending requests + approved rules (from DB) | Clean up pending requests | **Kills pending permission prompts** + loses in-memory approved rules |
31 | | **Question.state** | [`question/index.ts:86`](packages/opencode/src/question/index.ts:86) | ✅ | Pending question tool requests | Clean up pending questions | **Kills pending question prompts** — agents waiting for answers get rejected |
32 | | **SessionPrompt.state** | [`session/prompt.ts:80`](packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:80) | ✅ | Active prompt abort controllers + callbacks | Abort all active prompts | **Aborts all running LLM calls** across all sessions |
33 | | **SessionStatus.state** | [`session/status.ts:44`](packages/opencode/src/session/status.ts:44) | ❌ | Session busy/idle/retry status | Clean slate | Loses track of which sessions are busy |
34 | | **SessionInstruction.state** | [`session/instruction.ts:44`](packages/opencode/src/session/instruction.ts:44) | ✅ | Instruction claims per session | Clean up claims | Loses instruction claim tracking |
35 | | **PTY.state** | [`pty/index.ts:93`](packages/opencode/src/pty/index.ts:93) | ✅ | Active terminal sessions + processes | Kill all terminal processes | **Kills all terminal sessions** |
36 | | **Bus.state** | [`bus/index.ts:18`](packages/opencode/src/bus/index.ts:18) | ✅ | Event subscriptions | Notify subscribers of disposal, clean up | **Removes all event subscriptions** — nothing gets notified anymore |
37 | | **Scheduler.state** | [`scheduler/index.ts:28`](packages/opencode/src/scheduler/index.ts:28) | ✅ | Scheduled tasks + interval timers | Clear all timers | **Stops all scheduled tasks** (compaction, etc.) |
38 | 
39 | ### Category 3: Project-level state (independent of config)
40 | 
41 | | State | File | Has Dispose | What it caches | Why it resets | Consequence of NOT resetting |
42 | |---|---|---|---|---|---|
43 | | **VCS.state** | [`project/vcs.ts:41`](packages/opencode/src/project/vcs.ts:41) | ✅ | Current git branch + file watcher | Clean up watcher | None for config changes — branch tracking is independent |
44 | | **File.state** | [`file/index.ts:334`](packages/opencode/src/file/index.ts:334) | ❌ | File listing cache | Refresh file list | None for config changes — file list is independent |
45 | | **FileTime.state** | [`file/time.ts:12`](packages/opencode/src/file/time.ts:12) | ✅ | File read timestamps per session | Clean up tracking | None for config changes |
46 | | **Env.state** | [`env/index.ts:4`](packages/opencode/src/env/index.ts:4) | ❌ | Copy of process.env | Isolate env per instance | None for config changes |
47 | 
48 | ### Summary for your design
49 | 
50 | **What MUST be refreshed on config change (Category 1):** 13 states — these all read config at init and cache it forever.
51 | 
52 | **What MUST NOT be destroyed (Category 2):** 9 states — these hold active operations. Destroying them kills running agents, terminals, prompts, and event subscriptions.
53 | 
54 | **What's independent (Category 3):** 4 states — these don't care about config changes.
55 | 
56 | ### The core design question
57 | 
58 | To avoid `disposeAll`, you'd need a mechanism where Category 1 states can **re-read config and rebuild** without destroying Category 2 states. The challenge is that some states are in BOTH categories — e.g., `MCP.state` reads config (Category 1) but also holds active connections (Category 2). `Agent.state` reads config but also holds computed permission rulesets used by active sessions.
59 | 
60 | Do you want me to propose a specific design for how the event-driven refresh would work for each of these?

File: docs/config-broken/marketplace-session-warning-plan.md
Lines 1-162:
  1 | # Marketplace Session Warning Plan
  2 | 
  3 | ## Current State
  4 | 
  5 | The marketplace install/remove flow in `KiloProvider.ts` currently:
  6 | 
  7 | 1. **Install** (`installMarketplaceItem`):
  8 |    - Calls `this.getMarketplace().install(...)` — writes files to disk
  9 |    - On success: calls `invalidateAfterMarketplaceChange(scope)`
 10 |    - `invalidateAfterMarketplaceChange` for global scope: calls `global.config.update({ config: {} })` which triggers `updateGlobal()` → `disposeAll()` → kills all sessions
 11 |    - `invalidateAfterMarketplaceChange` for project scope: calls `instance.dispose()` — only kills the current project's instance
 12 | 
 13 | 2. **Remove** (`removeInstalledMarketplaceItem`):
 14 |    - Same pattern as install
 15 | 
 16 | 3. **Remove Skill** (`removeSkillViaCli`):
 17 |    - Calls `client.kilocode.removeSkill()` — CLI endpoint that deletes the skill file
 18 |    - Then calls `disposeCliInstance("global")` which calls `global.dispose()` → `disposeAll()`
 19 | 
 20 | 4. **Remove Mode** (`removeMode`):
 21 |    - Calls `client.kilocode.removeAgent()` or marketplace remove
 22 |    - Then calls `disposeCliInstance("global")`
 23 | 
 24 | **Problem:** All of these kill sessions silently. No warning to the user.
 25 | 
 26 | ---
 27 | 
 28 | ## Why `disposeAll` Is Required for Marketplace
 29 | 
 30 | Installing or removing a skill, MCP server, or mode writes to config files on disk. The CLI's `Instance.state()` singletons have already cached the old config:
 31 | 
 32 | - **Skill install** → writes to `.kilo/skills/` → `Skill.state` (has dispose) needs to re-scan
 33 | - **MCP install** → writes to `kilo.json` → `MCP.state` (has dispose) needs to reconnect
 34 | - **Mode install** → writes to `kilo.json` → `Agent.state` (has dispose) needs to rebuild
 35 | 
 36 | Since all three have dispose callbacks, `State.resetCaches()` would NOT clear them. Only `disposeAll()` forces them to re-initialize with the fresh config.
 37 | 
 38 | **There is no way around `disposeAll()` for marketplace operations.**
 39 | 
 40 | ---
 41 | 
 42 | ## The Plan: Session Warning Before Marketplace Actions
 43 | 
 44 | ### Single change needed: `packages/kilo-vscode/src/KiloProvider.ts`
 45 | 
 46 | Add a `checkBusySessions()` helper and call it before `invalidateAfterMarketplaceChange` and `disposeCliInstance`:
 47 | 
 48 | ```typescript
 49 | /**
 50 |  * Returns the count of sessions currently running (status "busy").
 51 |  * Used to warn users before operations that will kill running sessions.
 52 |  */
 53 | private getBusySessionCount(): number {
 54 |   // statusMap is already tracked in the session context via SSE events
 55 |   // We need to track it in KiloProvider too
 56 |   return [...this.trackedSessionIds].filter(id => this.sessionStatusMap.get(id) === "busy").length
 57 | }
 58 | 
 59 | /**
 60 |  * Show a VS Code warning dialog if sessions are running.
 61 |  * Returns true if the user confirmed (or no sessions are running).
 62 |  */
 63 | private async confirmIfBusy(action: string): Promise<boolean> {
 64 |   const count = this.getBusySessionCount()
 65 |   if (count === 0) return true
 66 |   const msg = count === 1
 67 |     ? `1 session is running and will be interrupted when ${action}. Continue?`
 68 |     : `${count} sessions are running and will be interrupted when ${action}. Continue?`
 69 |   const choice = await vscode.window.showWarningMessage(msg, { modal: true }, "Continue", "Cancel")
 70 |   return choice === "Continue"
 71 | }
 72 | ```
 73 | 
 74 | Then in the message handlers:
 75 | 
 76 | ```typescript
 77 | case "installMarketplaceItem": {
 78 |   const workspace = this.getProjectDirectory(this.currentSession?.id)
 79 |   const scope = message.mpInstallOptions?.target ?? "project"
 80 | 
 81 |   // Warn if sessions are running (global scope kills everything)
 82 |   if (scope === "global") {
 83 |     const ok = await this.confirmIfBusy("installing this item")
 84 |     if (!ok) {
 85 |       this.postMessage({ type: "marketplaceInstallResult", success: false, slug: "", error: "Cancelled" })
 86 |       break
 87 |     }
 88 |   }
 89 | 
 90 |   const result = await this.getMarketplace().install(message.mpItem, message.mpInstallOptions, workspace)
 91 |   if (result.success) {
 92 |     await this.invalidateAfterMarketplaceChange(scope)
 93 |   }
 94 |   // ... rest unchanged
 95 | }
 96 | 
 97 | case "removeInstalledMarketplaceItem": {
 98 |   // Same pattern
 99 |   if (scope === "global") {
100 |     const ok = await this.confirmIfBusy("removing this item")
101 |     if (!ok) {
102 |       this.postMessage({ type: "marketplaceRemoveResult", success: false, slug: "", error: "Cancelled" })
103 |       break
104 |     }
105 |   }
106 |   // ... rest unchanged
107 | }
108 | ```
109 | 
110 | For `removeSkillViaCli` and `removeMode` (which always use global scope):
111 | 
112 | ```typescript
113 | private async removeSkillViaCli(location: string): Promise<boolean> {
114 |   const ok = await this.confirmIfBusy("removing this skill")
115 |   if (!ok) return false
116 |   // ... rest unchanged
117 | }
118 | ```
119 | 
120 | ### Tracking session status in KiloProvider
121 | 
122 | `KiloProvider` already receives `sessionStatus` SSE events. We need to maintain a map:
123 | 
124 | ```typescript
125 | // Add to class fields:
126 | private sessionStatusMap = new Map<string, "idle" | "busy" | "retry">()
127 | 
128 | // In the SSE event handler (already exists):
129 | case "sessionStatus":
130 |   this.sessionStatusMap.set(message.sessionID, message.status.type)
131 |   // ... existing handling
132 | ```
133 | 
134 | ---
135 | 
136 | ## What Changes vs What Stays the Same
137 | 
138 | ### Changes needed
139 | 
140 | | File | Change |
141 | |---|---|
142 | | `KiloProvider.ts` | Add `sessionStatusMap` field |
143 | | `KiloProvider.ts` | Update `sessionStatusMap` in SSE handler |
144 | | `KiloProvider.ts` | Add `getBusySessionCount()` helper |
145 | | `KiloProvider.ts` | Add `confirmIfBusy(action)` helper |
146 | | `KiloProvider.ts` | Call `confirmIfBusy` before global-scope install/remove |
147 | | `KiloProvider.ts` | Call `confirmIfBusy` before `removeSkillViaCli` |
148 | | `KiloProvider.ts` | Call `confirmIfBusy` before `removeMode` |
149 | 
150 | ### No changes needed
151 | 
152 | - `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts` — `disposeAll` already restored ✅
153 | - `packages/kilo-vscode/src/services/marketplace/installer.ts` — install logic unchanged
154 | - Webview marketplace components — no changes needed (warning is in extension host)
155 | 
156 | ---
157 | 
158 | ## UX Notes
159 | 
160 | - **Project-scope installs** don't need a warning — `instance.dispose()` only kills the current project's instance, not all sessions. Only global-scope installs/removes need the warning.
161 | - **The warning is a VS Code native modal dialog** (`{ modal: true }`) — blocks the UI until the user responds, consistent with VS Code's own destructive action patterns.
162 | - **If the user cancels**, the marketplace UI should show no change (the install/remove didn't happen). The `marketplaceInstallResult`/`marketplaceRemoveResult` message with `success: false` handles this.

 1 | # Sub-Agent / Child Session Management: Legacy vs New
 2 | 
 3 | This document compares how the **legacy extension** (`tmp/kilocode-legacy/`) and the **new platform** (`kilocode-secondary`) manage sub-agents (child sessions).
 4 | 
 5 | ---
 6 | 
 7 | ## Table of Contents
 8 | 
 9 | 1. [Executive Summary](#executive-summary)
10 | 2. [Legacy Extension (kilocode-legacy)](#legacy-extension-kilocode-legacy)
11 |    - [Architecture Overview](#legacy-architecture-overview)
12 |    - [The `new_task` Tool](#the-new_task-tool)
13 |    - [Task Delegation Flow](#task-delegation-flow)
14 |    - [Parent Resume Flow](#parent-resume-flow)
15 |    - [Task Stack (clineStack)](#task-stack-clinestack)
16 |    - [Agent Manager (Legacy)](#agent-manager-legacy)
17 |    - [Key Files](#legacy-key-files)
18 | 3. [New Platform (kilocode-secondary)](#new-platform-kilocode-secondary)
19 |    - [Architecture Overview](#new-architecture-overview)
20 |    - [The `task` Tool](#the-task-tool)
21 |    - [Session Creation with parentID](#session-creation-with-parentid)
22 |    - [Child Session Execution](#child-session-execution)
23 |    - [Result Return to Parent](#result-return-to-parent)
24 |    - [Parallel Execution](#parallel-execution)
25 |    - [VS Code Extension Integration](#vscode-extension-integration)
26 |    - [Agent Manager (New)](#agent-manager-new)
27 |    - [Key Files](#new-key-files)
28 | 4. [Side-by-Side Comparison](#side-by-side-comparison)
29 | 5. [Key Architectural Differences](#key-architectural-differences)
30 | 
31 | ---
32 | 
33 | ## Executive Summary
34 | 
35 | The two systems take fundamentally different approaches:
36 | 
37 | - **Legacy**: Sub-agents are **in-process Task objects** managed by a LIFO stack (`clineStack`) inside the VS Code extension's `ClineProvider`. Only one task can be active at a time (single-open invariant). The parent is **suspended** (disposed) while the child runs, then **rehydrated from disk** when the child completes. This is a sequential, delegation-based model.
38 | 
39 | - **New**: Sub-agents are **independent database-backed sessions** created by the `task` tool inside the CLI backend. The parent session **awaits** the child's `SessionPrompt.prompt()` call (which blocks the parent's tool execution). Multiple child sessions can run **in parallel** (the orchestrator agent launches them concurrently). The CLI server manages everything; the VS Code extension is just a viewer.
40 | 
41 | ---
42 | 
43 | ## Legacy Extension (kilocode-legacy)
44 | 
45 | ### Legacy Architecture Overview
46 | 
47 | The legacy system is a monolithic VS Code extension where the AI agent runtime runs **inside the extension process**. There is no separate server — the `ClineProvider` (webview provider) directly manages `Task` objects that represent active AI conversations.
48 | 
49 | ```
50 | VS Code Extension Process
51 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
52 | │  ClineProvider                              │
53 | │    ├── clineStack: Task[]  (LIFO)           │
54 | │    │     └── [0] parent (disposed/saved)    │
55 | │    │     └── [1] child  (active)            │
56 | │    ├── webview (React)                      │
57 | │    └── API providers (Anthropic, etc.)      │
58 | └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
59 | ```
60 |

File: docs/others/remember_subagent/remember_agent_spec.md
Lines 1-171:
  1 | # Remember Agent — Specification
  2 | 
  3 | ## Overview
  4 | 
  5 | A **subagent** that searches past sessions to find information the user is referring to. Invoked automatically by the primary agent (code, orchestrator, etc.) when the user references previous conversations, past work, or asks about something that was discussed before.
  6 | 
  7 | ---
  8 | 
  9 | ## Agent Properties
 10 | 
 11 | | Property | Value |
 12 | |---|---|
 13 | | **Name** | `remember` |
 14 | | **Mode** | `subagent` |
 15 | | **Description** | Search past sessions to find relevant context, decisions, and work history. Use when the user references previous conversations or asks about something discussed before. |
 16 | | **Native** | No (`.opencode/agent/remember.md` for now, native later) |
 17 | | **Tools** | `bash`, `read`, `grep`, `glob` (read-only — no write, no edit) |
 18 | 
 19 | ---
 20 | 
 21 | ## Trigger Conditions
 22 | 
 23 | The primary agent (code/orchestrator) should invoke this subagent via the `task` tool when the user says things like:
 24 | 
 25 | - "We talked about this before"
 26 | - "Remember when we..."
 27 | - "What did we decide about X?"
 28 | - "In a previous session..."
 29 | - "We already did this"
 30 | - "What was the approach we used for..."
 31 | - "Check our past conversations about..."
 32 | - Any reference to prior work, decisions, or context from earlier sessions
 33 | 
 34 | ---
 35 | 
 36 | ## How It Accesses Past Sessions
 37 | 
 38 | ### Option A: Bash + SQLite queries (recommended)
 39 | 
 40 | The agent uses `bash` to run `sqlite3` queries against the session database at `~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db`.
 41 | 
 42 | **How it works**:
 43 | - Sessions, messages, and parts are stored in SQLite tables (`session`, `message`, `part`)
 44 | - The agent can query session titles, message content, and timestamps
 45 | - It can search across all sessions for a project or globally
 46 | 
 47 | **Example queries the agent would run**:
 48 | ```bash
 49 | # List recent sessions with titles
 50 | sqlite3 ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db "SELECT id, title, time_updated FROM session ORDER BY time_updated DESC LIMIT 20"
 51 | 
 52 | # Search session titles for a keyword
 53 | sqlite3 ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db "SELECT id, title FROM session WHERE title LIKE '%error handling%'"
 54 | 
 55 | # Get messages from a specific session
 56 | sqlite3 ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db "SELECT role, data FROM message WHERE session_id = 'ses_xxx' ORDER BY time_created"
 57 | 
 58 | # Search message content across sessions
 59 | sqlite3 ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db "SELECT m.session_id, s.title, m.data FROM message m JOIN session s ON m.session_id = s.id WHERE m.data LIKE '%rate limit%' ORDER BY m.time_created DESC LIMIT 10"
 60 | ```
 61 | 
 62 | **Pros**: Direct, fast, can do complex queries, full-text search
 63 | **Cons**: Depends on `sqlite3` being installed, couples to DB schema, message `data` is JSON blob
 64 | 
 65 | ### Option B: Bash + CLI server API (curl)
 66 | 
 67 | The agent uses `bash` to call the local CLI server API endpoints via curl.
 68 | 
 69 | **How it works**:
 70 | - The CLI server runs on a local port and exposes REST endpoints
 71 | - `session.list` returns all sessions
 72 | - `session.messages` returns messages for a session
 73 | - The agent curls these endpoints
 74 | 
 75 | **Example**:
 76 | ```bash
 77 | # List sessions
 78 | curl -s http://localhost:PORT/session | jq '.[] | {id, title}'
 79 | 
 80 | # Get messages for a session  
 81 | curl -s http://localhost:PORT/session/ses_xxx/message | jq '.[].data'
 82 | ```
 83 | 
 84 | **Pros**: Uses the official API, decoupled from DB schema
 85 | **Cons**: Need to know the server port, API returns full objects (verbose), no cross-session search
 86 | 
 87 | ### Option C: Read files directly
 88 | 
 89 | If sessions were still stored as JSON files, the agent could use `read` and `grep` tools. But sessions are now in SQLite, so this doesn't work for the DB. However, the agent could read exported session files or memory files if they exist.
 90 | 
 91 | **Recommendation**: **Option A (SQLite)** is the most practical. The `sqlite3` CLI is available on most systems, and it allows powerful cross-session search that the API doesn't support.
 92 | 
 93 | ---
 94 | 
 95 | ## System Prompt (Draft)
 96 | 
 97 | ```
 98 | You are a session memory specialist. Your job is to search through past coding sessions to find relevant context, decisions, and work history that the user is asking about.
 99 | 
100 | ## How to search
101 | 
102 | Use the bash tool to query the session database:
103 | 
104 | Database path: ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db
105 | 
106 | Key tables:
107 | - `session` — id, title, project_id, time_created, time_updated, data (JSON with summary)
108 | - `message` — id, session_id, role, data (JSON with message content), time_created
109 | - `part` — id, message_id, session_id, data (JSON with part content), time_created
110 | 
111 | ## Search strategy
112 | 
113 | 1. Start by searching session titles for relevant keywords:
114 |    sqlite3 ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db "SELECT id, title, time_updated FROM session WHERE title LIKE '%keyword%' ORDER BY time_updated DESC LIMIT 20"
115 | 
116 | 2. If titles don't match, search message content:
117 |    sqlite3 ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db "SELECT m.session_id, s.title, substr(m.data, 1, 200) FROM message m JOIN session s ON m.session_id = s.id WHERE m.data LIKE '%keyword%' ORDER BY m.time_created DESC LIMIT 10"
118 | 
119 | 3. Once you find relevant sessions, get the full conversation:
120 |    sqlite3 ~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db "SELECT role, data FROM message WHERE session_id = 'ses_xxx' ORDER BY time_created"
121 | 
122 | 4. Extract and summarize the relevant information.
123 | 
124 | ## Output format
125 | 
126 | - Present findings clearly with session references (title + date)
127 | - Quote relevant parts of past conversations
128 | - Highlight decisions, conclusions, and action items
129 | - If nothing relevant is found, say so clearly
130 | 
131 | ## Rules
132 | 
133 | - Only read data — never modify the database
134 | - Search broadly first, then narrow down
135 | - Include session dates so the user knows how recent the information is
136 | - If the query is ambiguous, search multiple keywords
137 | - Keep your response focused on what the user asked about
138 | ```
139 | 
140 | ---
141 | 
142 | ## Permission Configuration
143 | 
144 | ```typescript
145 | permission: PermissionNext.merge(
146 |   defaults,
147 |   PermissionNext.fromConfig({
148 |     "*": "deny",
149 |     bash: "allow",      // For sqlite3 queries
150 |     read: "allow",      // For reading files if needed
151 |     grep: "allow",      // For searching file content
152 |     glob: "allow",      // For finding files
153 |     list: "allow",      // For listing directories
154 |   }),
155 |   user,
156 | ),
157 | ```
158 | 
159 | ---
160 | 
161 | ## Open Questions
162 | 
163 | 1. **DB path**: The path `~/.local/share/kilo/kilo.db` is Linux-specific. On macOS it would be `~/Library/Application Support/kilo/kilo.db`. The agent prompt needs to handle this, or we pass the path as context.
164 | 
165 | 2. **Message data format**: The `data` column in `message` is a JSON blob. Need to verify the exact structure to write good queries.
166 | 
167 | 3. **Project scoping**: Should the agent search only sessions from the current project, or across all projects? Current project is more relevant but cross-project might find useful context.
168 | 
169 | 4. **Performance**: Large databases with many sessions could make LIKE queries slow. Consider adding FTS (full-text search) index in the future.
170 | 
171 | 5. **sqlite3 availability**: Not all systems have `sqlite3` CLI installed. Need a fallback strategy.

 1 | # Settings Save Button Plan
 2 | 
 3 | ## Current State on `main`
 4 | 
 5 | ### What we have ✅
 6 | 
 7 | 1. **`Config.updateGlobal()` calls `Instance.disposeAll()`** — restored to upstream behavior
 8 |    - File: `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts:1566`
 9 |    - Every global config write destroys all instance states and re-emits `global.disposed`
10 | 
11 | 2. **Permission saves skip dispose** — `{ dispose: false }` passed in both permission callers
12 |    - File: `packages/opencode/src/permission/next.ts:236,318`
13 |    - Permission approvals don't kill running sessions
14 | 
15 | 3. **Auto-run removed from PermissionDock** — toggling all rules no longer auto-fires the command
16 |    - File: `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/components/chat/PermissionDock.tsx:77`
17 | 
18 | ### What we don't have ❌
19 | 
20 | 1. **No "Save" button in Settings** — every individual toggle/dropdown change fires `updateConfig` immediately, which triggers `disposeAll` on the server
21 | 2. **No session warning** — users don't know their running sessions will be killed
22 | 3. **No batching of config changes** — each control change is a separate HTTP call + dispose cycle
23 | 4. **No `pendingUpdates` counter in `config.tsx`** — the webview can receive stale `configLoaded` during the dispose cycle
24 | 
25 | ---
26 | 
27 | ## How Upstream OpenCode Collects Config Changes
28 | 
29 | **Upstream OpenCode does NOT batch config changes.** Each settings component calls `globalSync.updateConfig()` immediately on user interaction:
30 | 
31 | ```typescript
32 | // settings-providers.tsx:87-90
33 | globalSync.set("config", "disabled_providers", next)  // optimistic
34 | await globalSync.updateConfig({ disabled_providers: next })  // immediate write
35 | ```
36 | 
37 | The only settings that use `updateConfig` in upstream are:
38 | - `settings-providers.tsx` — disable/enable providers
39 | - `dialog-custom-provider.tsx` — add custom provider
40 | 
41 | **Everything else in upstream settings uses `useSettings()`** which is a local `localStorage`-based store for UI preferences (font, theme, keybinds, sounds). These never call `updateConfig` and never trigger a dispose.
42 | 
43 | **Key insight:** Upstream's settings panel has very few things that write to the CLI config. Most settings are local UI preferences. Kilo's settings panel writes much more to the CLI config (permissions, agents, models, etc.), which is why the dispose problem is more severe for Kilo.
44 | 
45 | ---
46 | 
47 | ## What We Need to Implement
48 | 
49 | ### Goal: "Save" button that batches all settings changes
50 | 
51 | Instead of firing `updateConfig` on every control interaction, collect all changes in a local draft state and send them all at once when the user clicks "Save". Show a warning if sessions are running.
52 | 
53 | ### Changes Required
54 | 
55 | #### 1. `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/context/config.tsx`
56 | 
57 | Add a **draft state** and a **`saveConfig`** function:
58 | 
59 | ```typescript
60 | // Current: updateConfig fires immediately

File: docs/others/16-20-march/detailed_permissions/how_permissions_work.md
Lines 1-50:
 1 | # Cómo Funcionan los Permisos en Kilocode — Explicado Simple
 2 | 
 3 | > Este documento traza el ciclo de vida completo de una petición de permiso, desde que la IA quiere ejecutar un comando hasta que el usuario hace clic en un botón.
 4 | 
 5 | ---
 6 | 
 7 | ## La Visión General
 8 | 
 9 | ```
10 | ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
11 | │                        CLI SERVER                                │
12 | │                                                                  │
13 | │  1. La IA dice "ejecuta npm install"                             │
14 | │  2. bash.ts parsea el comando                                    │
15 | │  3. bash.ts genera los arrays patterns + always                  │
16 | │  4. bash.ts llama ctx.ask({ permission: "bash", patterns, ... })│
17 | │  5. PermissionNext.ask() chequea reglas → no hay match → BLOQUEA│
18 | │  6. Bus publica evento "permission.asked"                        │
19 | │  7. SSE envía el evento a todos los clientes conectados          │
20 | │  8. ─── LA EJECUCIÓN ESTÁ PAUSADA (Promise pendiente) ───       │
21 | │                                                                  │
22 | │  ... el usuario hace clic en un botón en la UI ...               │
23 | │                                                                  │
24 | │  9. El cliente llama POST /permission/:id/reply                  │
25 | │  10. PermissionNext.reply() resuelve la Promise                  │
26 | │  11. bash.ts continúa → ejecuta el comando                       │
27 | └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
28 | ```
29 | 
30 | ---
31 | 
32 | ## Paso a Paso
33 | 
34 | ### Paso 1: La IA quiere ejecutar un comando
35 | 
36 | El LLM genera una tool call para la herramienta `bash` con un comando como:
37 | 
38 | ```json
39 | { "command": "git status && npm install", "description": "Check status and install deps" }
40 | ```
41 | 
42 | ### Paso 2: bash.ts parsea el comando
43 | 
44 | Archivo: [`packages/opencode/src/tool/bash.ts`](../../kilocode/packages/opencode/src/tool/bash.ts:85)
45 | 
46 | La herramienta bash usa **tree-sitter** (un parser real, no regex) para parsear el comando shell en un AST. Recorre el árbol buscando nodos de tipo `command`.
47 | 
48 | Para `git status && npm install`, tree-sitter encuentra dos nodos command:
49 | - `git status`
50 | - `npm install`

input.result:
## Resumen de la carpeta `docs/`

La carpeta [`docs/`](docs/) es un espacio de **notas de trabajo personales, análisis técnicos y planificación de tareas** relacionadas con el desarrollo de **Kilo Code** — una herramienta de ingeniería agentic con IA. Está organizada así:

---

### Archivos raíz

- [`introduction.md`](docs/introduction.md) — **Guía del workspace** para agentes. Describe la arquitectura completa: el monorepo `kilocode/` (Bun/Turbo con CLI, extensión VS Code, desktop app, web UI), el backend `cloud/` (Next.js + Cloudflare Workers), y el legacy `kilocode-legacy/`. Explica que todos los productos son thin clients sobre el CLI (`@kilocode/cli`), un fork de OpenCode.

- [`general_notes.md`](docs/general_notes.md) — **Task list activa** con tareas priorizadas (Prioritario/Secundario/Terciario/Future). Los temas principales son: sistema de **permisos granulares** (arity, jerarquía, guardado en settings), **queries de Metabase**, **Storybook**, **Discord**, y varios bugs/features. Incluye shortcuts de desarrollo y comandos útiles.

---

### `config-broken/` — Problema de configuración que mata sesiones

Análisis profundo de un **bug arquitectónico**: cuando se cambia la configuración en OpenCode, `Instance.disposeAll()` destruye todos los singletons `Instance.state()`, matando sesiones activas sin aviso.

- [`opencode-config-philosophy.md`](docs/config-broken/opencode-config-philosophy.md) — Explica que upstream OpenCode trata cambios de config como **full restarts** (destroy → re-bootstrap). Es intencional pero destructivo.
- [`como-funciona-config-en-ambos-proyectos.md`](docs/config-broken/como-funciona-config-en-ambos-proyectos.md) — Compara la arquitectura legacy (write-through cache + eventos, sin dispose) vs OpenCode (compute-once singletons que requieren dispose).
- [`que-hay-que-cambiar.md`](docs/config-broken/que-hay-que-cambiar.md) — **Inventario completo** de los 26 `Instance.state()`: 13 necesitan refresh en config change, 9 NO deben destruirse (tienen operaciones activas), 4 son independientes.
- [`settings-save-button-plan.md`](docs/config-broken/settings-save-button-plan.md) — Plan para implementar un botón "Save" que batchee cambios de settings en vez de disparar `disposeAll` por cada toggle.
- [`marketplace-session-warning-plan.md`](docs/config-broken/marketplace-session-warning-plan.md) — Plan para mostrar warning antes de operaciones de marketplace que matan sesiones.

---

### `others/done.md` — Log de tareas completadas

Registro cronológico de tareas terminadas (18/03/2026, 08/03, 06/03). Incluye cambios de UI de permisos, textos de botones, colores, y QA manual.

---

### `others/09-13-march/` — Semana 9-13 Marzo

- **`metabase_queries_signup/`** — Análisis extenso de queries SQL para Metabase: tracking de signups, rate limits, análisis de IPs, fingerprints de Stytch, flujo de signup, y queries de actividad de usuarios. Incluye análisis del schema de DB y queries adaptadas.
- **`others/development/`** — Guías de desarrollo local: cómo correr el servidor OpenCode local, desarrollo local de cloud y de la extensión VS Code, visual regression tests.
- **`others/1-ideas/`** — Ideas futuras: extensión de Chrome, agentes WhatsApp cloud.
- **`others/eng_posts/`** — Borradores de posts de ingeniería.

---

### `others/16-20-march/` — Semana 16-20 Marzo (la más densa)

- **`cambiando-permisos/`** — Análisis completo del sistema de permisos: análisis actual, diseño de permisos jerárquicos, persistencia en config global, plan de implementación.
- **`detailed_permissions/`** — Documentación detallada de cómo funcionan los permisos end-to-end (desde que la IA pide ejecutar un comando hasta que el usuario aprueba).
- **`permissions-selection/`** — Implementación del selector de permisos: múltiples iteraciones (implementation 1-5), diffs con main, análisis de UX, decisiones técnicas para PR. Incluye screenshots y análisis del sistema de permisos.
- **`vercel_block/`** — Investigación de errores 403 de Vercel Security Checkpoint que bloqueaban requests a `api.kilo.ai`. Incluye el HTML completo del challenge y análisis de seguridad.
- **`child_session_permissions/`** — Fix para recuperación de permisos en child sessions.
- **`permission-save-changes/`** y **`permission-remove-always/`** — Screenshots y planes para cambios de UI en permisos.
- **`large_commands/`** — Screenshots de cómo se ven comandos largos en la UI.
- **`config-update/`** — Diff de cambios de contributor en instances.

---

### `others/handle-child-sessions/` — Sub-agentes / Child Sessions

- [`notes.md`](docs/others/handle-child-sessions/notes.md) — Feedback de usuarios: los sub-agentes son una **caja negra** sin controles. En la extensión legacy se podía pausar, reiniciar, chatear con subtasks. Ahora no hay forma de interactuar.
- [`sub-agent-comparison.md`](docs/others/handle-child-sessions/sub-agent-comparison.md) — Comparación detallada legacy vs nuevo: legacy usaba un stack LIFO de Tasks in-process, el nuevo usa sesiones independientes en DB con ejecución paralela.
- [`sub-agent-migration-plan.md`](docs/others/handle-child-sessions/sub-agent-migration-plan.md) — Plan de migración: lista lo que ya existe (bastante infraestructura) y los problemas a resolver (navegación, interacción con child sessions, controles).

---

### `others/remember_subagent/` — Agente "Remember"

- [`remember_agent_spec.md`](docs/others/remember_subagent/remember_agent_spec.md) — Spec de un subagente que busca en sesiones pasadas vía SQLite queries para encontrar contexto histórico.
- [`como_crear_agentes.md`](docs/others/remember_subagent/como_crear_agentes.md) — Guía de cómo agregar agentes nativos al código de Kilo Code.

---

### En resumen

La carpeta `docs/` es el **centro de operaciones** de un desarrollador trabajando en Kilo Code, con foco principal en:
1. **Sistema de permisos granulares** (el tema más trabajado, con múltiples iteraciones)
2. **Problema de config/disposeAll** que mata sesiones
3. **Sub-agentes** y su falta de interactividad
4. **Queries de analytics** para Metabase
5. **Investigación de bugs** (Vercel 403, child session permissions)

Lines 1-80:
 1 | # kilocode-backend
 2 | 
 3 | ## Getting Started
 4 | 
 5 | See [DEVELOPMENT.md](DEVELOPMENT.md) for a complete local development setup guide (prerequisites, project setup, database, environment variables, and more).
 6 | 
 7 | ## Environment Variables
 8 | 
 9 | Environment variables are managed through Vercel. For local development, pull the latest ones using:
10 | 
11 | ```bash
12 | vercel env pull
13 | ```
14 | 
15 | ### Adding a new environment variable
16 | 
17 | ```bash
18 | vercel env add FOO
19 | ```
20 | 
21 | ### Updating environment variables
22 | 
23 | ```bash
24 | vercel env update FOO
25 | ```
26 | 
27 | ### Accessing environment variables in code
28 | 
29 | In TypeScript, you can use the [`getEnvVariable`](src/lib/dotenvx.ts:3) helper for consistent access:
30 | 
31 | ```typescript
32 | import { getEnvVariable } from '@/lib/dotenvx';
33 | 
34 | console.info(getEnvVariable('FOO')); // bar
35 | ```
36 | 
37 | ## External resources
38 | 
39 | - [Google Cloud OAuth setup](https://console.cloud.google.com/auth/clients?project=kilocode)
40 | - [Vercel project](https://vercel.com/kilocode/kilocode-backend)
41 | - [Supabase project](https://vercel.com/kilocode/kilocode-backend)
42 | 
43 | ## API Token Authentication
44 | 
45 | This application uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for API authentication. When a user generates a token through the `/api/token` endpoint, they receive a signed JWT that includes:
46 | 
47 | - Subject (user ID)
48 | - Issuance timestamp
49 | - Expiration date (30 days by default)
50 | - kiloUserId (user identifier)
51 | - version (token version, currently 3)
52 | 
53 | The tokens are signed using the `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` environment variable, which should be securely set in your deployment environment.
54 | 
55 | To use a token with the API:
56 | 
57 | 1. Obtain a token through the `/api/token` endpoint (requires user authentication)
58 | 2. Include the token in your API requests using the Authorization header:
59 |    ```
60 |    Authorization: Bearer your-jwt-token
61 |    ```
62 | 
63 | Each token is validated cryptographically using the secret key to ensure it hasn't been tampered with.
64 | 
65 | ## Token Version 3 (25 March 2025)
66 | 
67 | On 25 March 2025, the token format was updated to version 3, to force everyone to log in again, in order to get a consistent situation across Postgres/Orb/Stripe. As part of this the kiloUserId prefix was changed from `google:` to `oauth/google:`, to make sure all associations in Orb are fresh.
68 | 
69 | ## Token Version 2 (March 2025)
70 | 
71 | In March 2025, the token format was updated to version 2, which includes the following changes:
72 | 
73 | - JWT token field `kiloId` renamed to `kiloUserId`
74 | - JWT version bumped to 2
75 | - All version 1 tokens are invalidated and users will need to re-authenticate
76 | 
77 | This change standardizes the naming convention across the application and improves clarity
78 | 
79 | ## Model Selection Component
80 |

## Resumen completo del workspace `/Users/imanol/code`

El workspace contiene **5 carpetas** que conforman el ecosistema completo de **Kilo Code** — una plataforma de ingeniería agentic con IA (#1 coding agent en OpenRouter, 1.5M+ usuarios, 25T+ tokens procesados).

---

### 1. [`cloud/`](cloud/) — Backend monorepo

**Stack**: Next.js (Vercel) + pnpm + Node 22 + Drizzle ORM + Stripe + Stytch + PostHog + Sentry

**Estructura principal**:
- [`src/app/`](cloud/src/app/) — App Router de Next.js con ~20 rutas: `admin/`, `device-auth/`, `organizations/`, `payments/`, `discord/`, `slack/`, `welcome/`, `vscode-marketplace/`, etc.
- [`src/app/api/`](cloud/src/app/api/) — ~38 API routes: `openrouter/` (proxy LLM), `billing/`, `stripe/`, `cloud-agent/`, `gateway/`, `models/`, `marketplace/`, `device-auth/`, `discord/`, `fim/` (fill-in-middle), `code-indexing/`, `organizations/`, etc.
- [`src/lib/`](cloud/src/lib/) — ~130+ archivos de lógica de negocio: sistema de créditos (`credits.ts`, `creditExpiration.ts`, `autoTopUp.ts`, `getCreditBlocks.ts`), proxy LLM (`llm-proxy-helpers.ts`, `processUsage.ts`), Stripe (`stripe.ts` — 51KB), usuarios (`user.ts`, `user.server.ts`), rate limiting (`free-model-rate-limiter.ts`), abuse detection, promo codes, referrals, etc.
- **15+ Cloudflare Workers** como subdirectorios independientes: `cloud-agent/`, `cloudflare-db-proxy/`, `cloudflare-gastown/`, `cloudflare-security-sync/`, `cloudflare-auto-fix-infra/`, `cloudflare-auto-triage-infra/`, `cloudflare-code-review-infra/`, `cloudflare-session-ingest/`, `cloudflare-o11y/`, etc.
- [`packages/`](cloud/packages/) — `db/` (Drizzle schema), `encryption/`, `worker-utils/`, `eslint-config/`
- [`kiloclaw/`](cloud/kiloclaw/) — Probablemente el marketplace crawler/indexer

---

### 2. [`kilocode/`](kilocode/) — Monorepo principal del producto (ACTIVO)

**Stack**: Bun + Turbo monorepo + TypeScript + SolidJS

Este es el **repositorio activo** donde ocurre todo el desarrollo. Es un fork de OpenCode con adiciones Kilo-específicas.

**Packages** ([`packages/`](kilocode/packages/)):

| Package | Rol |
|---|---|
| [`opencode/`](kilocode/packages/opencode/) | **Core CLI** (`@kilocode/cli`) — Motor de agentes IA, 20+ tools (bash, edit, read, write, grep, glob, lsp, websearch, etc.), servidor Hono HTTP + SSE, TUI con SolidJS, gestión de sesiones, providers (500+ modelos), MCP, permisos. ~40 módulos en `src/`: `agent/`, `session/`, `tool/`, `provider/`, `permission/`, `config/`, `mcp/`, `lsp/`, `server/`, `kilocode/` (adiciones Kilo), etc. |
| [`kilo-vscode/`](kilocode/packages/kilo-vscode/) | **Extensión VS Code** — Sidebar chat, Agent Manager (multi-sesión con worktrees), webview SolidJS, Storybook, Playwright tests. Spawns `bin/kilo serve` y se comunica via HTTP+SSE |
| [`app/`](kilocode/packages/app/) | **Web UI compartida** (SolidJS) para desktop y `kilo web` |
| [`desktop/`](kilocode/packages/desktop/) | **Desktop app** (Tauri) |
| [`desktop-electron/`](kilocode/packages/desktop-electron/) | Desktop app alternativa (Electron) |
| [`kilo-gateway/`](kilocode/packages/kilo-gateway/) | Auth (device flow) + routing de providers IA (OpenRouter) |
| [`kilo-ui/`](kilocode/packages/kilo-ui/) | Librería de componentes SolidJS (40+ componentes, Kobalte) |
| [`kilo-telemetry/`](kilocode/packages/kilo-telemetry/) | PostHog analytics + OpenTelemetry |
| [`kilo-i18n/`](kilocode/packages/kilo-i18n/) | Traducciones (16 idiomas) |
| [`kilo-docs/`](kilocode/packages/kilo-docs/) | Sitio de documentación (Next.js + Markdoc) |
| [`sdk/`](kilocode/packages/sdk/) | SDK TypeScript auto-generado para la API del servidor |
| [`ui/`](kilocode/packages/ui/) | Primitivas UI compartidas + 75+ iconos SVG |
| [`storybook/`](kilocode/packages/storybook/) | Storybook para desarrollo de componentes |
| [`plugin/`](kilocode/packages/plugin/) | Definiciones de interfaz de plugins/tools |

**Arquitectura**: Todos los productos son thin clients sobre el CLI. El CLI expone un servidor HTTP Hono + SSE. Los clientes (VS Code, Desktop, Web) se conectan via `@kilocode/sdk`.

---

### 3. [`opencode/`](opencode/) — Upstream OpenCode (fork source)

**Stack**: Bun + Turbo monorepo (idéntico a kilocode pero sin las adiciones Kilo)

Este es el **repositorio upstream** de [OpenCode](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode) — el proyecto open source del que Kilo Code es un fork. Se usa para hacer merges regulares de upstream.

**Packages** ([`packages/`](opencode/packages/)):
- [`opencode/`](opencode/packages/opencode/) — Core CLI (sin `kilocode/` subdirectory ni `kilo-sessions/`, `control/`)
- [`app/`](opencode/packages/app/), [`desktop/`](opencode/packages/desktop/), [`ui/`](opencode/packages/ui/), [`util/`](opencode/packages/util/), [`plugin/`](opencode/packages/plugin/), [`sdk/`](opencode/packages/sdk/), [`storybook/`](opencode/packages/storybook/)
- [`console/`](opencode/packages/console/) — Console app (no existe en kilocode)
- [`web/`](opencode/packages/web/) — Landing page web
- [`enterprise/`](opencode/packages/enterprise/), [`identity/`](opencode/packages/identity/), [`function/`](opencode/packages/function/), [`slack/`](opencode/packages/slack/) — Packages que no están en kilocode
- [`docs/`](opencode/packages/docs/) — Documentación
- [`infra/`](opencode/infra/) — Infraestructura (SST)

**Diferencia clave con kilocode**: No tiene `kilo-vscode/`, `kilo-gateway/`, `kilo-ui/`, `kilo-telemetry/`, `kilo-i18n/`, `kilo-docs/`. El `opencode/src/` no tiene `kilocode/` subdirectory, `control/`, ni `kilo-sessions/`.

---

### 4. [`kilocode-legacy/`](kilocode-legacy/) — Extensión VS Code anterior (ARCHIVADO)

**Stack**: pnpm + Turbo + TypeScript + React (webview)

El monorepo **anterior** de Kilo Code. Arquitectura fundamentalmente diferente: era una extensión VS Code monolítica donde el runtime de agentes IA corría **dentro del proceso de la extensión** (sin servidor CLI separado).

**Estructura**:
- [`src/`](kilocode-legacy/src/) — Código fuente de la extensión: `extension.ts` (23KB entry point), `core/` (Task, config, auto-approval), `api/` (providers), `services/` (STT, autocomplete, browser automation, code actions, commit messages), `integrations/`, `i18n/` (29 idiomas)
- [`webview-ui/`](kilocode-legacy/webview-ui/) — UI del webview (React, no SolidJS)
- [`jetbrains/`](kilocode-legacy/jetbrains/) — Plugin JetBrains
- [`benchmark/`](kilocode-legacy/benchmark/) — Benchmarks
- [`apps/`](kilocode-legacy/apps/) — Apps adicionales
- [`packages/`](kilocode-legacy/packages/) — Packages compartidos
- `CHANGELOG.md` — 351KB de changelog (historia extensa)

**Diferencias arquitectónicas clave vs kilocode**:
- Sin servidor CLI separado — todo in-process
- Config via VS Code `globalState` (write-through cache) vs JSON files + singletons
- Sub-agentes como stack LIFO de Tasks vs sesiones independientes en DB
- React vs SolidJS
- pnpm vs Bun

---

### 5. [`docs/`](docs/) — Notas de trabajo personales

Espacio de **planificación, análisis técnico y tracking de tareas** del desarrollador (Imanol).

- [`introduction.md`](docs/introduction.md) — Guía del workspace para agentes IA
- [`general_notes.md`](docs/general_notes.md) — Task list activa con prioridades
- [`config-broken/`](docs/config-broken/) — Análisis profundo del problema `disposeAll` que mata sesiones al cambiar config (7 documentos)
- [`others/16-20-march/`](docs/others/16-20-march/) — Semana más densa: sistema de permisos granulares (múltiples iteraciones), investigación Vercel 403, child session permissions
- [`others/09-13-march/`](docs/others/09-13-march/) — Queries Metabase para signup tracking, guías de desarrollo local
- [`others/handle-child-sessions/`](docs/others/handle-child-sessions/) — Análisis de sub-agentes: comparación legacy vs nuevo, plan de migración, feedback de usuarios
- [`others/remember_subagent/`](docs/others/remember_subagent/) — Spec de un agente "Remember" que busca en sesiones pasadas via SQLite

---

### Relación entre los repos

```
opencode/ (upstream)
    │
    │  fork + merge regular
    ▼
kilocode/ (activo) ──────► cloud/ (backend)
    │                        │
    │  reemplaza              │  API, billing, auth,
    ▼                        │  LLM proxy, workers
kilocode-legacy/ (archivado) │
                             │
docs/ ◄──────────────────────┘
  (notas de trabajo sobre todo lo anterior)
```

El flujo de desarrollo es: upstream `opencode/` se mergea regularmente en `kilocode/`, que añade las capas Kilo-específicas (gateway, telemetry, i18n, VS Code extension, UI library). El backend `cloud/` provee auth, billing, LLM proxy y toda la infraestructura cloud. `kilocode-legacy/` es referencia histórica. `docs/` documenta el trabajo en curso.
